# RGT Codebase Scanner — LLM usage guide
# Run `rgt-scan --tool-schema` for machine-readable JSON.

Tool: rgt-scan
Aliases: omni-scan (legacy)
Version: 9.0.0
Purpose: Scan codebases, files, URLs, Docker images, and environment variables
         for secrets, PII, high-entropy tokens, and prompt injection.

## Modes

quick  No extra dependencies. Regex-based secrets, PII, entropy analysis, git
       history. Runs in under 5 seconds on most repos. Use this by default.

full   Requires `pip install "rgt-codebase-scanner[all]"`. Adds NLP/Presidio
       PII detection, AST context filtering, Semgrep SAST, perplexity and
       homoglyph analysis, taint flow, steganography detection, external tools
       (gitleaks, trivy), and watch mode.

## Entry point

rgt-scan [OPTIONS] [PATH]

## Key flags

--target-type {repo,path,url,docker,env,clipboard}
    What to scan. Default: repo (local git repository).
    path    = file or directory tree
    url     = fetch raw content from URL
    docker  = scan Docker image layers
    env     = scan current process environment variables
    clipboard = read from system clipboard

--format {text,json,sarif,html}
    Output format. Default: text. Use json or sarif for programmatic use.

--output FILE
    Write report to FILE instead of stdout.

--pii            Enable regex-based PII detection.
--nlp            Enable spaCy NLP PII detection (requires [nlp] extra).
--presidio       Enable Microsoft Presidio PII (requires [presidio] extra).
--injection      Enable prompt-injection pattern detection.
--entropy-threshold FLOAT
                 Minimum Shannon entropy to flag a token. Default: 4.5.
--mask           Mask secret values in output.
--quiet          Suppress warnings and progress messages.
--all-branches   Scan all git branches, not just HEAD.
--exclude PATTERN
                 Skip paths matching PATTERN (glob).
--tool-schema    Print OpenAI/Anthropic function-calling JSON schema and exit.
--self-test      Run built-in self-test and exit.

## Typical invocations

Scan current repo (quick):
    rgt-scan

Scan a directory:
    rgt-scan --target-type path /path/to/dir

Scan a URL:
    rgt-scan --target-type url https://example.com/raw/file.txt

Scan Docker image:
    rgt-scan --target-type docker myimage:latest

Scan env vars:
    rgt-scan --target-type env

JSON output to file:
    rgt-scan --format json --output findings.json

SARIF for GitHub Code Scanning:
    rgt-scan --format sarif --output results.sarif

Full scan with NLP:
    rgt-scan --pii --nlp --presidio --injection --mask

## Configuration file

Place `.omni-scan.toml` in the repo root for persistent settings:

    [scanner]
    entropy_threshold = 4.5
    mask = true

    [exclude]
    patterns = ["*.lock", "node_modules/"]
    tokens   = ["EXAMPLE", "placeholder"]

    [custom_patterns]
    secrets = [{ name = "Corp Key", pattern = "corp_[A-Za-z0-9]{32}" }]

## Output schema (--format json)

{
  "secrets":     [ { "file": str, "line": int, "type": str, "value": str } ],
  "pii":         [ { "file": str, "line": int, "type": str, "value": str } ],
  "high_entropy":[ { "file": str, "line": int, "token": str, "entropy": float } ],
  "history":     { "<commit>": [ ... ] },
  "summary":     { "total_findings": int, "files_scanned": int }
}

## Part of the RGT suite

rgt-scan is one tool in the RGT suite. Other tools share the same
`.omni-scan.toml` configuration convention and `rgt-` CLI prefix.
